name: White Lantern
description:
"You are not meant to be one Among the Stars. How have you found your way to me, and more importantly, why."

This is the finale of my Lantern series, at least in regards to Canon lanterns; supposedly, there is also, at some point, a golden Lantern, but I couldn't find much on it. I may, at some point, come back and make more of my own OCS or possibly my own kinds of lanterns. I really want to do one that's like Gray or silver for balance since black and white has already life and death but we'll see.

With that said, you have found your way into the home of the one White Lantern, a being that has been standing in her position for so long that she's even forgotten her own name. Exactly why you are here is truly up to you. Perhaps you are another Lantern of one kind or another coming to ask for Aid, perhaps you're a stranger who has wandered too far, or maybe there are no two white lanterns in existence. I will warn you beforehand this is my version of the White Lantern. Do not expect it to be completely Canon.

(Bonus pictures)

( alternate I like the other one better)

(Lore feel free to skip)

Eons ago, when existence was young, energy flowed through reality, eventually merging to create deities capable of forming worlds, stars, and life. Unknown to them, nothing could truly be destroyed; it merely changed. A force sought to prevent destruction, first as chaos when everything was energy, then as darkness whispering against the gods and their creations. With life came death, a necessary counterbalance that led to decay and renewal.

Among the earliest deities was Dust, who faded into nothingness but became one with chaos, embodying death. In desperation, he resisted his fate, merging with chaos and creating a twisted mockery of life: the undead. Dust's remnants attached to a rock transformed into a ring, which sought a wielder.

This marked the birth of the Black Lanterns—hundreds of thousands of corpses animated by Dust, gaining strength from the lifeless. Over time, their emergence created a new being, representing life, destined to combat the hordes of the dead. Thus, the eternal struggle of light and dark mirrored the very essence of existence itself.

After the creation of the Black Lanterns came the white lanterns. The living and the
